provenance.notes: Processed 4 quotes from 5 verse files. Source: HistoricalChristianFaith/Commentaries-Database. License: PUBLIC DOMAIN.

data.author: Liturgy of Saint Mark
data.quote: Give him the victory, O God, that his heart may be set on peace and the praise of Your holy name, that we too in his peaceful reign may spend a calm and tranquil life in all reverence and godly fear, through the grace, mercy, and love of Your only-begotten Son.
data.source_title: Divine Liturgy of St. Mark, Section IX

data.author: Liturgy of Saint Mark
data.quote:
O Lord our God, give peace to the souls of our fathers and brethren who have fallen asleep in Jesus, remembering our forefathers of old, our fathers, patriarchs, prophets, apostles, martyrs, confessors, bishops, and the souls of all the holy and just men who have died in the Lord.

Especially remember those whose memory we this day celebrate, and our holy father Mark, the apostle and evangelist, who has shown us the way of salvation.
data.source_title: Divine Liturgy of St. Mark, Section XIV

data.author: Liturgy of Saint Mark
data.quote: Remember in Thy good mercy the Holy and only Catholic and Apostolic Church throughout the whole world, and all Thy people, and all the sheep of this fold.
data.source_title: Divine Liturgy of St. Mark, Section XIV

data.author: Liturgy of Saint Mark
data.quote: The Deacon: Bow your heads to Jesus.
data.source_title: Divine Liturgy of St. Mark, Section XIX
